description:
Implement balls-plugin-github as a separate binary using the existing plugin protocol (README §Plugin System). Commands:

- auth-setup: prompt for GitHub token (PAT or fine-grained), validate against GET /user, write to .balls/local/plugins/github/token.json.
- auth-check: re-validate token, exit 0/1.
- push --task ID: for a task in status=review under delivery.mode=deferred — open or update the PR for work/bl-xxxx against the configured target_branch. PR title includes [bl-xxxx]. Store pull_request: { number, url, head_sha, target_branch } in task.external.github.
- sync [--task ID]: for each deferred-mode task in review with task.external.github.pull_request set, poll PR state via GitHub API. When state=merged, return an "updated" entry that closes the gate child (the one auto-opened by bl review in deferred mode), carrying the merge_commit_sha in the add_note field. Core processes this through the normal sync-report path; the gate child closes; the parent's bl close becomes unblocked.

WHY: the lifecycle hooks landed by the deferred-squash and close-resolution balls work without any forge plugin — humans can drive it manually by closing the gate child themselves after merging the PR via the GitHub UI. The plugin automates the close-the-gate step so agents don't have to babysit forge state, and centralizes [bl-xxxx]-tag enforcement on PR title.

Auth and API specifics stay entirely in the plugin per balls's principle "plugins extend, core stays small" (README §Principles).

Out of scope for this ball:
- GitLab/Bitbucket — separate balls if there's demand. The lifecycle work is forge-agnostic; only the plugin is GitHub-specific.
- Automating the initial PR open. The plugin can support it (push --task), but agents may also use gh pr create directly; both paths are valid.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Plugin binary balls-plugin-github passes auth-check against a valid PAT.
- push --task on a deferred-mode review task opens a PR; task.external.github populated.
- sync detects a merged PR and emits a sync-report that closes the gate child with the merge SHA in the note.
- After plugin sync, the gate child is closed; bl close on the parent succeeds and resolves delivered_in via tag-scan.
title: balls-plugin-github: forge-gated delivery automation
